PERIODONTITIS- smoking is a major risk factor for increasing the
prevalence and severity of periodontal destruction.
- Pocket depth, attachment loss, alveolar boneloss
are more prevalent and severe in patients who smoke
- On average, smokers were 4 times as likely to
have penodontitis as compared who had never smoked
- Former smokers were 1.7 times more likely to have
periodontitis than persons who had never smoked.
- older adult smokers are approx. 3 times more
likely to have severe periodontal disease and the no:
of years of tobacco use is a significant factor in:-
tooth loss,
coronal root carles,
periodontaldiscase
- smoking has also been shown to affect periodontal
disease severity in younger individuals.
- smokers are more than 6hmes as likely as non-smokers
to demonstrate continued attachment loss.
- Former smokers have less risk for periodontitis
than current smokers.
suggests that negative effects of smoking an the
